description: Project-based computer science education inspired by 42 Network peer-to-peer pedagogy. No professors — learn by doing and peer code review. Completed projects spanning systems programming, networking, compilers, graphics, and Docker infrastructure.
Started programming during the pandemic lockdown. Learned Python, JavaScript, and HTML/CSS from scratch, building small projects to gain foundational software development skills.

Studied mechanical engineering — thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, material science, and CAD-based design. This engineering foundation directly shapes how I approach software architecture and systems design.
Specialized training in CNC machine operation and programming for aerospace parts. Gained hands-on precision manufacturing and CAD/CAM experience.
Technical diploma in mechanical manufacturing. Learned industry-standard CAD tools including Catia and SolidWorks for design, prototyping, and production planning.
Graduated with strong focus on mathematics and physics. Built the analytical problem-solving mindset that underpins my approach to complex engineering challenges.
